titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 760

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=999 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.02737+00 | translation moa=998 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.057654+00

p.cl: Dauid Comes . Iohanni episcopo . et Cospatrico . et Colbano . et Rodberto . et omnibus suis fidelibus Tegnis et dringis de lodeneio et de Teuegetedale . Salutem

ego concedo Monachis Sancti Cuthberti cum omnibus consuetudinibus omnia que habebant tam in terris quam in aquis . die qua frater meus Eadgarus uiuus
et mortuus fuit . et sicut ego ipse domino meo sancto Cuthberto post mortem illius fratris mei eisdem Monachis concessi . Et sicut frater meus Eadgarus Sancto Cuthberto
et Monachis suis dedit Swintunam et super altare in Coldingeham obtulit  ita eandem omnino totam ex mea parte dono et concedo Sancto predicto
et monachis eius liberam et quietam tenendam et amodo habendam sicut breue fratris mei predicti eis testatur . Et precipio omnibus meis hominibus ut nullus
eis amodo aliquam inde faciat molestiam uel iuniuriam . quia uolo ut hanc meam donationem in pace et honorifice habeant et teneant et in perpetuum possideant .
testibus Mathildis reginę . et Willelmi filii sui . et Iohannis episcopi .
David the earl, to John the bishop, Cospatric, Colban, Robert, and all his sworn men, thanes and drengs of Lothian and Teviotdale, greeting.

I grant to the monks of saint Cuthbert with all customs everything which they had as well in lands as in waters on the day when my brother Edgar was alive and dead, and just as I myself have granted to my lord Saint Cuthbert after my brother's death to the same monks; and just as my brother Edgar gave, and offered upon the altar of Coldingham, Swinton to Saint Cuthbert and his monks, so for my part

I present as a gift and grant the same entire and whole to the aforesaid saint and his monks, to be held free and undisturbed, and to be had in future just as the brieve of my aforesaid brother for them bears witness. And I instruct all my men that no one shall in future make any trouble or do any injustice in respect of it, since I will that they should have, hold, and possess this my donation in peace for ever.
As witness, Matilda the queen, William her son, and John the bishop.
